controls.label: Does your business have a Verification Standards — Electronic process?
controls.remediation: Your organisation does not have a Verification Standards — Electronic process in place. You must establish a documented Verification Standards — Electronic process to meet your AML/CTF obligations. Review the controls below and implement the required procedures before completing this assessment.

controls.label: Does your AML/CTF program provide for verification of KYC information using reliable and independent electronic data?
controls.remediation: Your Verification Standards – Electronic process must establish whether reliable and independent electronic data will be used for KYC verification. If you do not have documented procedures for electronic data verification, you cannot comply with the requirements in rule 4.10.1 which mandates systems and controls for this verification method.

controls.label: Have you established criteria to assess the accuracy of the electronic data sources you use for verification?
controls.remediation: Your electronic data verification process must include risk-based systems and controls to determine whether electronic data is reliable and independent. Your assessment must evaluate data accuracy as required under rule 4.10.2(1)(a)—specify the accuracy standards or error rates you accept for electronic verification sources.

controls.label: Have you established criteria to assess the security of the electronic data sources you use?
controls.detail-label: Describe your data security assessment criteria
controls.remediation: Your electronic data verification process must include risk-based systems and controls to determine whether electronic data is reliable and independent. Your assessment must evaluate data security as required under rule 4.10.2(1)(b)—document the security standards you require for electronic verification sources (e.g., encryption, access controls, audit trails).

controls.label: Have you established criteria to assess how regularly the electronic data sources you use are kept up-to-date?
controls.detail-label: Describe your data currency assessment criteria
controls.remediation: Your electronic data verification process must include risk-based systems and controls to determine whether electronic data is reliable and independent. Your assessment must evaluate how data is kept up-to-date as required under rule 4.10.2(1)(c)—specify acceptable update frequencies or refresh intervals for the electronic sources you rely on.

controls.label: Have you established criteria to assess the comprehensiveness of the electronic data sources you use (such as the range of persons included and the collection period)?
controls.detail-label: Describe your data comprehensiveness assessment criteria
controls.remediation: Your electronic data verification process must include risk-based systems and controls to determine whether electronic data is reliable and independent. Your assessment must evaluate data comprehensiveness as required under rule 4.10.2(1)(d)—document how you evaluate the range of persons covered and the historical period of data in your electronic sources.

controls.label: Have you established criteria to verify that the electronic data has been verified from a reliable and independent source?
controls.remediation: Your electronic data verification process must include risk-based systems and controls to determine whether electronic data is reliable and independent. Your assessment must verify that the data itself comes from a reliable and independent source as required under rule 4.10.2(1)(e)—document how you confirm the provenance and independence of your electronic data sources.

controls.label: Have you established criteria to assess whether the electronic data is maintained by a government body or pursuant to legislation?
controls.detail-label: Describe your government/legislative data assessment criteria
controls.remediation: Your electronic data verification process must include risk-based systems and controls to determine whether electronic data is reliable and independent. Your assessment must consider whether data is maintained by a government body or pursuant to legislation as required under rule 4.10.2(1)(f)—document how you identify and prioritise such authoritative sources.

controls.label: Have you established criteria to assess whether the electronic data can be additionally authenticated?
controls.remediation: Your electronic data verification process must include risk-based systems and controls to determine whether electronic data is reliable and independent. Your assessment must evaluate the potential for additional authentication of the data as required under rule 4.10.2(1)(g)—specify the authentication methods (e.g., multi-source cross-checking, third-party validation) you employ.

controls.label: Have you pre-defined which reliable and independent electronic data sources your organisation will use for verification purposes?
controls.detail-label: List or describe the electronic data sources you will use
controls.remediation: Your electronic data verification process must establish systems and controls to determine what reliable and independent electronic data will be used as required under rule 4.10.2(2). You must pre-define and document the specific electronic data sources your reporting entity will rely on for KYC verification.

controls.label: Have you established pre-defined tolerance levels for matches and errors when using electronic data for verification?
controls.detail-label: Describe your pre-defined tolerance levels for matches and errors
controls.remediation: Your electronic data verification process must include risk-based systems and controls to determine the reporting entity's pre-defined tolerance levels for matches and errors as required under rule 4.10.2(3). Document the specific thresholds (e.g., % match required, acceptable error rates) that will trigger acceptance or rejection of electronic verification results.

controls.label: Have you established procedures to determine when, and how, to confirm KYC information by independently initiating contact with the person the customer claims to be?
controls.detail-label: Describe your independent contact confirmation procedures
controls.remediation: Your electronic data verification process must establish risk-based systems and controls as required under rule 4.10.2(4) to decide whether and how to independently confirm KYC information by contacting the person the customer claims to be. Document your decision criteria (e.g., risk triggers, customer types) and the methods you will use for independent confirmation.

groups.title: Process Confirmation
groups.description: Before answering the detailed compliance questions, confirm whether your organisation operates this business process.

groups.title: Electronic Verification Scope
groups.description: Determination of whether your AML/CTF program will use reliable and independent electronic data for KYC verification.

groups.title: Assessment of Electronic Data Reliability
groups.description: Risk-based systems and controls to determine whether electronic data is reliable and independent, evaluating accuracy, security, currency, comprehensiveness, source provenance, government/legislative origin, and additional authentication capabilities.

groups.title: Pre-Defined Electronic Data Sources
groups.description: Selection and documentation of the specific reliable and independent electronic data sources your organisation will use for verification.

groups.title: Tolerance Levels and Error Thresholds
groups.description: Pre-defined tolerance levels for matches and acceptable errors when using electronic data verification.

groups.title: Independent Confirmation Procedures
groups.description: Risk-based procedures to determine when and how to independently confirm KYC information by directly contacting the person the customer claims to be.
